data.narrative:
HyImpulse is a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Christian Schmierer, Ulrich Fischer, Mario Kobald, and Konstantin Tomilin. The company has raised €74 million to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace, supplemented by 5 public sector comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) for benchmarking context.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. Three Series B private comparables—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M)—yield a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public Space & Aerospace comps trade at a median P/S of 42.9x across 6 tickers; however, this method cannot be applied given HyImpulse's undisclosed revenue figures, which is typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, centered on a median of $1.3B. This estimate rests on a single applicable method, introducing material uncertainty around peer selection and the representativeness of the three comparables. Key variables include HyImpulse's revenue trajectory relative to peers, capital efficiency, and whether the peer set adequately reflects the target's technology differentiation and market position. Wider dispersion among the three comps ($400M–$2.0B spread) signals considerable variance in how the market prices Series B aerospace companies.
